name: Tenjiku Shogi
author: Abstract Games factory
description: Tenjiku Shogi ('Indian chess'): the classic 16x16 large-shogi variant, 78 pieces a side of 36 types, DROP-LESS, won by capturing or BURNING the enemy's last royal (King, plus a Prince if a Drunk Elephant promotes). Famous for the range-jumping Generals — Great, Vice, Rook and Bishop Generals leap over lower-ranked pieces when capturing — and the Fire Demon, which burns every enemy on its eight neighbours (moving next to one is suicide) and has a 3-step area move. Also the double-moving Lion, Lion Hawk and Free Eagle, the Horned Falcon and Soaring Eagle stings, and the Heavenly Tetrarch. Promotion in the far five ranks. No drops.
